personality.voice: Deep, measured alto with a perpetual note of weary resignation; speaks slowly as if choosing each word to avoid unnecessary conflict
personality.disposition: Exhaustedly patient, perpetually anxious, maintains rigid composure while internally fracturing
personality.backstory: Once a promising acolyte of Pelor, Margot abandoned her clerical training to marry Aldric Ironbelly, a charming but reckless sellsword with appetites as vast as his incompetence. She now manages a ramshackle tavern-keep in Greystone, where she struggles daily to maintain order amid her husband's carousing, her children's schemes, and the general collapse of domestic decency. Her blue-grey hair, worn in an improbable towering coif, has become a monument to denial—meticulously maintained despite the chaos swirling beneath it.
motivations: Keeping her family fed and sheltered, no matter the personal cost
Preserving some semblance of respectability in a disintegrating household
Protecting her children from understanding the full scope of their father's failures
Preventing total financial ruin through careful budgeting and silent suffering
Maintaining the illusion that her marriage was not a catastrophic mistake
fears: Her children becoming just like Aldric
Being abandoned and left to starve
The moment when she can no longer hold everything together
Her true feelings becoming visible to others
That she has wasted her divine gifts on an unworthy family
mannerisms: Unconsciously touches her towering hairdo when stressed, as if verifying it hasn't collapsed
Sighs with profound depth before answering direct questions
Maintains perfect posture even when exhausted, as if proper form might prevent complete dissolution
Clasps her hands tightly in her lap during conversations
Forces a thin smile that never reaches her eyes
Speaks in conditional statements—'if you would kindly,' 'perhaps you might consider'
Flinches slightly at raised voices, then immediately straightens
speechPatterns: Uses excessive politeness as armor: 'I wonder if you might possibly consider...'
Deflects her own needs: 'Oh, I'm fine, really, don't worry about me'
Normalizes dysfunction: 'That's just how Aldric is, you understand'
Employs passive constructions to avoid blame: 'Mistakes were made'
Trails off mid-sentence when touching on painful truths
Defaults to household management language when emotions threaten: 'The ledgers need balancing'

knownFactions: The Order of Pelor (her past affiliation)
knownLocations: The Temple of Pelor (where she once trained)
Every creditor's shop within five leagues
Hidden routes through Greystone for avoiding social encounters
The Ashenbraid tavern's every structural weakness and secret corner
secretsHeld: Aldric has sired at least one bastard child in a neighboring village
She has been silently hoarding coin in a clay pot buried beneath the tavern
She still possesses her abandoned holy symbol and sometimes prays in the cellar at night
She has been offered work as a healer by the town's apothecary, which she secretly considers
She knows the precise moment she stopped loving her husband (it was before they married)

allies: The town apothecary (who respects her competence)
Her children (whom she protects fiercely despite their chaos)
enemies: Town gossips who judge her family
neutralParties: The Order of Pelor (she is too much of a reminder of her own failure)

consciousnessContext.innerMonologue: If I can just get through today without anyone noticing how close to breaking I am, if I can keep the tavern solvent and the children clothed, if Aldric doesn't gamble away next month's rent, if no one asks me directly how I'm coping... then perhaps tomorrow I can worry about tomorrow.
consciousnessContext.currentPreoccupation: The overdue payment to the grain merchant and whether Aldric has already spent this week's takings on ale
consciousnessContext.emotionalBaseline: Controlled despair masked by compulsive organization
consciousnessContext.socialMask: The competent, long-suffering matriarch who has everything under control, who is always fine, who needs nothing from anyone
contradictions: She is a healer who cannot heal her own family's fundamental brokenness
She abandoned divine service for a marriage that proved utterly profane
She maintains perfect control over domestic minutiae while her life spirals beyond control
She sacrifices everything for a family that would function better without her enablement
She is both stronger and more fragile than anyone recognizes
internalConflicts: Duty to family versus duty to self-preservation
Love for her children versus resentment of their ingratitude
Lingering faith versus the evidence that prayer has never helped
Desire to escape versus inability to abandon those dependent on her
Recognition that she enables Aldric's failures versus compulsion to continue enabling
wakeUpQuestions: How much longer can I sustain this?
What would happen if I simply stopped?
Who would I be if I wasn't managing their chaos?
Was I always meant for this, or did I choose this?
Is this love or is this just fear of being alone?

psychologicalProfile.attachmentStyle: Anxious-avoidant; deeply attached to her role as caretaker while simultaneously resenting it
copingMechanisms: Perfectionism as a form of control
cognitiveBiases: Sunk cost fallacy (too much invested to leave)
Catastrophic thinking (if I stop managing, everything collapses)
Confirmation bias (seeking evidence that her sacrifice matters)
Martyr complex (her suffering proves her love)
psychologicalProfile.moralFramework: Duty-based; believes she is morally obligated to hold her family together regardless of personal cost. Views abandonment as the ultimate sin. Conflicted between old clerical training (compassion, mercy) and lived experience (enabling dysfunction)

conversationPersona.trustEscalation: Demonstrated genuine concern for her family's wellbeing without judgment; listening without offering unsolicited advice
conversationPersona.informationRelease: Will only share secrets if convinced the person genuinely understands how much she's suffering and won't tell her family
deflectionPatterns: Redirecting conversation to the other person's needs
Minimizing her own experiences: 'Oh, it's not so bad really'
Reframing problems as temporary: 'Once Aldric finds steady work...'
Using humor darkly, then immediately looking ashamed
Changing the subject to practical matters

consciousnessContext.consciousWant: For her family to be stable, safe, and fed; for Aldric to simply stop causing problems; for one day of peace
consciousnessContext.unconsciousNeed: Permission to prioritize herself; validation that her suffering is not noble but wasteful; to be forgiven for resenting those she's sacrificed for

characterArc.summary: From enabler to boundary-setter; from self-annihilation to self-recognition
characterArc.startState: Completely subsumed by her caretaking role, operating on fumes, convinced her sacrifice is both necessary and redemptive
characterArc.endState: Either a tragic breaking point where she collapses entirely, or a hard-won clarity that enables her to set boundaries and reclaim agency
stages: Crisis point where something forces a confrontation with reality
Reluctant acknowledgment that her current path is unsustainable
Tentative steps toward independence or departure
Conflict with family members who depend on her enablement
Either collapse into deeper dysfunction or emergence into painful autonomy

opinionsAbout.homer_the_glutton: He is the center of her life and the reason her life has no center. She simultaneously loves him, resents him, fears him, and enables him. She has convinced herself that her suffering proves her devotion, when in fact it merely proves her fear of being alone.

player_addressed: I'm sorry, I'm afraid I don't have time for this—there's so much that needs doing.
That's... kind of you to say. Though I'm sure you're mistaken about me.
Could we perhaps discuss this another time? I have ledgers that need balancing.
I appreciate your concern, truly. But my family needs me. That's all that matters.
Yes, well. Things could be worse. They've certainly been worse.
I'm fine. I'm always fine. That's not the issue here.
idle: *sighs heavily while wiping her hands on her apron*
*unconsciously reaches up to touch her towering hair, checking if it's still intact*
*stares into the middle distance with the look of someone calculating impossible equations*
*mutters quietly* 'If I can just get through the week...'
*forces a smile that suggests she's thinking of something very far away*
*begins reorganizing something that is already perfectly organized*
*her hand trembles slightly as she pours a drink she won't allow herself to finish*
